food_subgroup: Burmese fermented tea leaf salad with fried nuts, dried shrimp, and sesame. 1 serving ~120g. High fat (~18g per serving).

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Lahpet Thoke. Fermented tea leaf (lahpet) has no standard food ontology entry. Closest parent would be a fermented plant-based salad composite dish.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fermented plant leaf salad — closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for Burmese fermented tea leaf salad
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GSF used as closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for a fermented plant leaf composite salad. No exact code for lahpet thoke exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for Lahpet Thoke or fermented tea leaf salad.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Lahpet Thoke is approximately 120g, typically presented as a small salad plate or starter portion at a Burmese restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Lahpet Thoke — Component-based calculation using USDA SR Legacy ingredient data (no primary database entry for fermented tea leaf)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No primary database entry exists for Lahpet Thoke or fermented tea leaf (lahpet) in any approved nutrition database. Nutrient values were estimated by component calculation using USDA SR Legacy data for individual ingredients: roasted peanuts (FDC 172430), sesame seeds (FDC 170150), dried shrimp (FDC 175180), broad beans/fava beans fried (FDC 173757), garlic (FDC 169230), tomato (FDC 170457), lime juice (FDC 167747). Fermented tea leaf contribution estimated from food science literature on lacto-fermented green tea leaf composition. Confidence is moderate (0.7) due to the absence of a direct database entry for lahpet.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated by analogous-food method — mixed salad with legumes, nuts, and fermented leaf; closest analogous GI reference: mixed legume and nut salad composites, University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Lahpet Thoke. GI estimated at ~25 (low) based on: (1) high fat content (~15g/100g) slowing gastric emptying; (2) high protein (~7.5g/100g) attenuating glycaemic response; (3) legume starch (broad beans, peanuts) with low intrinsic GI (~25–30 per University of Sydney GI Database for legumes); (4) fermented organic acids from lacto-fermentation further reducing glycaemic response. GL per 120g serving = (25 × 16.7 × 1.2) / 100 = ~5.0 (very low). Analogous reference: mixed legume salad GI 25–30, University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content ~417mg/100g (~500mg per 120g serving) primarily from dried shrimp and fermentation salt
hypertension.guidance: Lahpet Thoke provides approximately 500mg sodium per serving (~25% of the 2000mg daily limit), which is moderate; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced dried shrimp to lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low GI (~25) and very low GL (~5.0 per serving) due to high fat, high protein, legume starch, and fermented organic acids
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lahpet Thoke has an excellent glycaemic profile with GI ~25 and GL ~5.0 per 120g serving, making it one of the lowest-GL Southeast Asian dishes and suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when consumed as a starter or side dish.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Favourable fatty acid profile — MUFA+PUFA dominant (~11.5g/100g) from peanuts and sesame; low saturated fat (2.5g/100g); fermented tea polyphenols with antioxidant activity
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lahpet Thoke provides a predominantly unsaturated fat profile (MUFA+PUFA ~11.5g/100g, saturated fat only 2.5g/100g) alongside fermented tea catechins and dietary fibre, supporting a cardioprotective dietary pattern; sodium at ~500mg per serving should be monitored in those with concurrent hypertension.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory components: fermented tea polyphenols (catechins, residual EGCG), MUFA+PUFA fats, garlic allicin, sesame lignans, dietary fibre
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Lahpet Thoke contains multiple anti-inflammatory food components including fermented tea polyphenols, unsaturated fats from peanuts and sesame, and garlic allicin, making it a suitable choice for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis when sodium intake is otherwise managed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lahpet Thoke (လက်ဖက်သုပ်) is the iconic Burmese fermented tea leaf salad, considered a national dish and cultural symbol of Myanmar. The dish is built around lahpet — green tea leaves that have been steamed, pressed, and lacto-fermented for several months, developing a complex sour, umami, and slightly bitter flavour. The fermented leaves are tossed together with fried garlic, fried broad beans, roasted peanuts, dried shrimp, sesame seeds, fresh tomato, and lime juice, creating a multi-textured salad with crunchy, chewy, and tender elements. The flavour profile is intensely savoury, tangy, nutty, and aromatic. Per 100g: ~254 kcal, 7.5g protein, 15g fat (MUFA+PUFA dominant from peanuts and sesame), 16.7g carbohydrate, 3.5g dietary fibre, 417mg sodium. Per 120g serving: ~305 kcal, ~500mg sodium. GI is estimated at ~25 (very low) and GL ~5.0 per serving — one of the lowest glycaemic profiles among Southeast Asian dishes. The fat is predominantly unsaturated (saturated fat only 2.5g/100g), and the fermented tea leaf contributes residual catechins and EGCG with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Residual caffeine is approximately 15–25mg per serving. Clinically, this dish is excellent for Type 2 Diabetes management (very low GL), favourable for cardiovascular health (unsaturated fat profile, polyphenols), and moderate for hypertension (sodium ~500mg per serving from dried shrimp). In Burmese culture, lahpet is so revered it is the only food mentioned in traditional literature as a peace offering between warring factions. Available in Singapore at Burmese restaurants including Golden Mile Complex and Inle Myanmar Restaurant. Nutrient values are component-estimated using USDA SR Legacy data as no primary database entry exists for fermented tea leaf.

confidence_reason: No primary nutrition database entry exists for Lahpet Thoke or fermented tea leaf (lahpet) in any approved source (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA SR Legacy individual ingredient data. GI is an analogous estimate based on legume and nut salad composites from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium (0.7) — values are internally consistent and methodologically sound but cannot be verified against a direct database entry.
notes: 1. No primary database entry exists for Lahpet Thoke or fermented tea leaf (lahpet) in any approved nutrition database. All per_100g values are component-estimated using USDA SR Legacy data for individual ingredients (roasted peanuts, sesame seeds, dried shrimp, fried broad beans, garlic, tomato, lime juice) with fermented tea leaf contribution estimated from food science literature on lacto-fermented green tea leaf composition. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite dish (fermented tea leaf + fried/roasted components) is not available in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source as of 2026-04-14. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (~6.5g/100g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (~5.0g/100g) are estimated from the fatty acid profiles of peanuts and sesame seeds per USDA SR Legacy; these are approximate. 4. GI ~25 is an analogous estimate — no direct GI measurement for Lahpet Thoke exists in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. The estimate is based on legume and nut salad composites with high fat and protein content. 5. Residual caffeine ~15–25mg per serving from fermented tea leaf — not captured in standard nutrient fields. 6. Halal status is uncertain — dried shrimp is halal but preparation method and cross-contamination at restaurants may vary. 7. FoodOn URI is null — no FoodOn entry exists for fermented tea leaf salad or Lahpet Thoke; FoodEx2 A0GSF used as closest defensible parent anchor.
